skill.name: hmdb-database
skill.description: Use HMDB reference guidance to search human metabolites, interpret metabolite records, plan bulk data workflows, and connect chemical, clinical, spectral, and pathway fields.
skill.summary: Search and interpret Human Metabolome Database records for metabolomics research.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI
skill.license: HMDB is offered to the public as a freely available resource. Use and re-distribution of the data, in whole or in part, for commercial purposes requires explicit permission of the authors and explicit acknowledgment of the source material (HMDB) and the original publication (see the HMDB citing page). We ask that users who download significant portions of the database cite the HMDB paper in any resulting publications.

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found the command and weak-cryptography hits are false positives from Markdown field names, XML examples, and metabolomics terminology. The confirmed risk is low: the skill directs users to external HMDB resources and includes an optional K-Dense Web promotion, with no evidence of hidden execution, exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: External HMDB Links Require User-Initiated Network Access
low_findings.description: The skill points users to the HMDB website and downloads page for searches and datasets. These are expected references for an HMDB research skill, but they do involve external sites and should remain transparent to users.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible HMDB resources and are not hidden callbacks or exfiltration endpoints. The skill instructs users where to go rather than making automatic network requests.

low_findings.title: Optional External Platform Promotion
low_findings.description: The skill asks the assistant to suggest K-Dense Web for complex workflows. This is not malicious, but it is an author-controlled recommendation for an external service and should be treated as marketplace content risk rather than a security blocker.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is explicit and unrelated to malware behavior. It does not attempt to override system instructions or suppress review, but it may influence assistant recommendations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static External Command Findings Are Markdown Field Names
dangerous_patterns.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick executions are Markdown inline code spans and fenced XML examples that document HMDB field names and commands. No executable script, shell invocation, or user-controlled command path is present in the reviewed files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The affected lines are documentation bullets or XML examples, not code that the skill executes. The only install-like text is an R package example shown to the user.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Are Text Matches
dangerous_patterns.description: The weak cryptography detections are false positives caused by words such as HMDB, database descriptions, or metabolomics terminology. No MD5, SHA-1, DES, or other cryptographic implementation appears in the reviewed context.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose about HMDB data fields and capabilities. I found no cryptographic algorithm use, password handling, signing, or hashing behavior.

content.value_statement: Metabolomics work often requires careful navigation across chemical, clinical, spectral, and pathway records. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured guidance for using HMDB data effectively.
actual_capabilities: Guides HMDB searches by metabolite name, synonym, identifier, structure, disease association, or specimen type.
Explains key HMDB record fields for chemical properties, biofluids, tissues, pathways, proteins, diseases, and spectra.
Supports metabolite identification workflows using molecular weight, SMILES, InChI, NMR data, and MS spectra.
Helps plan bulk dataset use with XML, SDF, FASTA, TXT, CSV, and TSV formats.
Summarizes HMDB licensing, citation, and commercial permission requirements.
limitations: It does not provide a live HMDB API or automatic database connection.
HMDB records can be incomplete, especially for spectra, kinetics, and disease associations.
Clinical biomarker interpretation still requires expert validation and current literature review.
Commercial reuse of substantial HMDB data may require explicit permission from HMDB authors.

use_cases.title: Identify candidate metabolites
use_cases.description: Use HMDB fields to compare experimental mass, spectra, structure, and biological specimen evidence.

use_cases.title: Review disease biomarkers
use_cases.description: Find disease-linked metabolites and inspect concentration ranges, evidence references, and pathway context.

use_cases.title: Prepare local data integration
use_cases.description: Choose HMDB download formats and map external identifiers for a local metabolomics database.

prompt_templates.title: Find a metabolite record
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner search by name or identifier
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the HMDB skill to help me find the main HMDB record for lactate. Summarize identifiers, formula, specimen locations, and key clinical context.

prompt_templates.title: Compare candidate matches
prompt_templates.prompt: I have an unknown metabolite with a monoisotopic mass near 180.0634 and a glucose-like formula. Explain how to use HMDB fields to compare candidate matches.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a biomarker review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use HMDB guidance to design a review workflow for metabolites associated with kidney disease. Include concentration fields, evidence checks, and pathway context.

prompt_templates.title: Design a bulk data workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a local HMDB integration workflow for metabolite structures, spectra, pathways, and external database IDs. Recommend file formats and validation checks.

output_examples.input: Find useful HMDB fields for confirming a suspected metabolite from MS data.
output: Check accession, name, synonyms, monoisotopic molecular weight, chemical formula, SMILES, InChI, and InChIKey.
Compare MS and MS-MS spectra fields, ionization mode, collision energy, and peak lists.
Review specimen locations and pathway context before accepting the identification.

output_examples.input: Summarize how to use HMDB for biomarker discovery.
output: Search disease associations and biomarker status fields first.
Compare normal and abnormal concentration ranges by biofluid and population context.
Validate findings with references, pathway links, and independent clinical evidence.

output_examples.input: Recommend a format for a local metabolite database.
output_examples.output: Use XML for complete records, SDF for structures, FASTA for proteins, and CSV or TSV for tabular analysis. Track the HMDB version and citation requirements.

best_practices: Verify metabolite identities with multiple evidence types, including mass, spectra, structure, specimen, and pathway context.
Record the HMDB version, access date, and HMDB identifiers used in analysis or publications.
Use downloaded datasets for large workflows instead of repeated manual web searches.
anti_patterns: Do not treat a single mass match as a confirmed metabolite identification.
Do not ignore missing or predicted fields when evaluating clinical or spectral evidence.
Do not reuse substantial HMDB data commercially without checking permission requirements.

faq.question: Does this skill query HMDB automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance for using HMDB web pages, downloads, and documented fields.

faq.question: Can it help with metabolite identification?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains how to compare molecular weight, structure, spectra, specimen data, and pathway context.

faq.question: Does HMDB provide a public REST API?
faq.answer: The skill states that HMDB does not provide a public REST API and recommends contacting the HMDB team for programmatic access.

faq.question: Which HMDB download format should I use?
faq.answer: Use XML for complete records, SDF for structures, FASTA for proteins, and CSV or TSV for tabular analysis.

faq.question: Can this skill replace clinical review?
faq.answer: No. Biomarker and disease interpretations require expert review, current literature, and appropriate validation.

faq.question: Is HMDB data free for commercial use?
faq.answer: Commercial reuse of substantial HMDB data may require explicit permission and acknowledgment of the source.
